findings.message: New user-facing Dashboard view fetches /internal/summary with no RUM/error instrumentation reachable from it. The fetch chain (fetch -> r.json() -> setData) has no .catch and the component has no error boundary, so a failed or non-2xx /internal/summary response is silently swallowed: a non-ok HTTP status does not reject the promise, and r.json()/setData failures never reach the js-errors counter or any RUM signal. The view's failure behavior is completely unobserved.
findings.suggestion: Add an explicit error path that surfaces the failure to RUM: check res.ok and on failure (and in a .catch) increment the frontend js-errors signal / rethrow so the window 'error'/'unhandledrejection' handler in observability/rum.ts records app_frontend_js_errors_total, and wrap the view in an error boundary so render failures are observed.
